skill.name: discord-automation
skill.description: Automate Discord messages, direct messages, roles, webhooks, and reactions through Rube MCP and Composio toolkits.
skill.summary: Guides AI agents through Discord automation workflows using Rube MCP tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external-command, weak-crypto, and system-reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and Discord tool names. The skill has medium operational risk because it uses a third-party MCP endpoint and documents Discord actions that can send messages, alter roles, remove reactions, and execute webhooks.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint and Discord Authorization
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authorize Discord or Discordbot connections. This is legitimate setup guidance, but it sends Discord automation through an external service and should be reviewed before use in sensitive servers.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external MCP URL and authorization flow are directly documented. The behavior appears intentional and legitimate, but it expands trust to a third-party service.

medium_findings.title: Discord Administrative Actions Can Change Server State
medium_findings.description: The skill documents workflows for creating, assigning, deleting, and updating roles, plus removing reactions. These are expected Discord administration capabilities, but they can disrupt a server if used with excessive permissions or without confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences include role deletion, member updates, and reaction deletion. The skill also notes permission requirements and destructive outcomes, confirming the operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Webhook Token Handling Requires Secret Protection
medium_findings.description: The skill documents webhook execution with a webhook_token parameter and correctly warns that webhook tokens are secrets. Exposure of those tokens could let another party post through the webhook.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token parameter and secret-handling warning are explicitly documented. No exfiltration is shown, but the data type is sensitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backticked strings as Ruby or shell backtick execution. In context, these are Markdown-formatted MCP tool names, field names, and Discord parameters, not executable local code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanned file is Markdown documentation, and the backticked text appears inside bullets and tables. I found no script body, shell invocation, or user input command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms Are Discord API Lookups
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged quick-reference rows such as list, get, and test auth operations as reconnaissance. These entries refer to Discord resources through named MCP tools, not local host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are a Markdown table of Discord tool slugs and parameters. No evidence found of local system inventory, process enumeration, or host data collection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm on the front matter description line. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or encryption routine is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a natural-language description of Discord automation. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use in that line.

dangerous_patterns.title: Discord Administrative Actions Can Change Server State
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents workflows for creating, assigning, deleting, and updating roles, plus removing reactions. These are expected Discord administration capabilities, but they can disrupt a server if used with excessive permissions or without confirmation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented tool sequences include role deletion, member updates, and reaction deletion. The skill also notes permission requirements and destructive outcomes, confirming the operational risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Webhook Token Handling Requires Secret Protection
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents webhook execution with a webhook_token parameter and correctly warns that webhook tokens are secrets. Exposure of those tokens could let another party post through the webhook.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token parameter and secret-handling warning are explicitly documented. No exfiltration is shown, but the data type is sensitive.

content.value_statement: Discord automation often requires many tool calls and exact identifiers. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through messages, roles, webhooks, and reactions with Rube MCP.
actual_capabilities: Lists Discord guilds and channels before message workflows.
Sends and edits channel messages through Discordbot tools.
Creates direct message channels and sends direct messages.
Creates, assigns, removes, and updates Discord roles.
Creates, lists, updates, and executes Discord webhooks.
Lists and removes message reactions by emoji or user.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Discord or Discordbot connection.
Depends on Discord permissions granted to the bot or user account.
Does not bypass Discord rate limits, privacy settings, or blocked direct messages.
Requires current tool schemas from RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS before execution.

use_cases.title: Post Scheduled Server Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and send announcements to selected channels while checking channel IDs and message limits first.

use_cases.title: Manage Member Roles
use_cases.description: Create roles, assign them to members, and verify hierarchy or permission constraints before changes.

use_cases.title: Run Integration Webhooks
use_cases.description: Create or execute Discord webhooks for external alerts while treating webhook tokens as secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Channel Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a simple post
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Discord automation skill to find my guild and channel, then send this message: [message]. Confirm the channel before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Direct Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or find a DM channel for user [user ID], then send this message: [message]. Stop if the user cannot receive DMs.

prompt_templates.title: Assign a Role Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the member and role IDs in guild [guild ID], verify role hierarchy requirements, then assign role [role ID] to user [user ID].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Webhook Alert Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or use a webhook in channel [channel ID] for alert messages. Protect the webhook token and send a test alert with clear content.

output_examples.input: Send an announcement to the events channel.
output_examples.output: The agent lists guilds, lists channels, asks you to confirm the events channel, then sends the approved announcement.

output_examples.input: Give a member the moderator role.
output_examples.output: The agent checks the guild, member, and role identifiers, verifies role hierarchy guidance, then applies the role if permissions allow it.

output_examples.input: Post a deployment alert through a webhook.
output_examples.output: The agent finds or creates the webhook, keeps the token out of logs, and sends the alert content to the selected channel.

best_practices: Search current Rube tool schemas before every workflow.
Confirm guild, channel, user, role, and webhook identifiers before changing server state.
Use least-privilege Discord permissions and protect webhook tokens.
anti_patterns: Sending bulk messages without respecting Discord rate limits.
Granting administrator permissions when a narrower permission is enough.
Pasting webhook tokens into prompts, logs, or shared transcripts.

faq.question: Does this skill include a Discord bot?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance for using Discord and Discordbot tools through Rube MCP.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: You need Rube MCP connected and an active Discord or Discordbot connection through Composio.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes, if the bot can create the DM channel and the recipient allows messages.

faq.question: Can it manage roles?
faq.answer: Yes, when the bot has Manage Roles permission and the target role is below the bot role.

faq.question: Does it protect webhook tokens automatically?
faq.answer: No. The workflow warns that webhook tokens are secrets, but users must handle them securely.

faq.question: Can it avoid Discord rate limits?
faq.answer: No. It instructs agents to respect Retry-After headers and Discord route limits.
